# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    nginx \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    postgresql \
    redis-server \
    supervisor \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install a simple web server (e.g., Nginx serving static content)
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/default
RUN mkdir -p /var/www/html
RUN echo "<h1>Hello from Nginx!</h1>" > /var/www/html/index.html

# Install a simple Python API server (Flask example)
COPY requirements.txt /app/requirements.txt
COPY api_server.py /app/api_server.py
RUN pip3 install -r /app/requirements.txt

# Configure PostgreSQL
RUN service postgresql start && \
    su - postgres -c "psql -c \"CREATE USER myuser WITH PASSWORD 'mypass';\"" && \
    su - postgres -c "psql -c \"CREATE DATABASE mydb OWNER myuser;\"" && \
    service postgresql stop

# Supervisor configuration
COPY supervisord.conf /etc/supervisor/conf.d/supervisord.conf

EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

CMD ["/usr/bin/supervisord", "-c", "/etc/supervisor/conf.d/supervisord.conf"]

# --- Helper files to be copied into the Docker image ---

# nginx.conf (place this file next to your Dockerfile)
# server {
#     listen 80;
#     server_name localhost;
#     root /var/www/html;
#     index index.html;
#     location / {
#         try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
#     }
# }

# requirements.txt (place this file next to your Dockerfile)
# Flask
# psycopg2-binary
# redis

# api_server.py (place this file next to your Dockerfile)
# from flask import Flask
# import psycopg2
# import redis
# import os

# app = Flask(__name__)

# @app.route('/')
# def hello():
#     return "Hello from Flask API!"

# @app.route('/db')
# def test_db():
#     try:
#         conn = psycopg2.connect(
#             host="localhost",
#             database="mydb",
#             user="myuser",
#             password="mypass"
#         )
#         cur = conn.cursor()
#         cur.execute("SELECT 1;")
#         cur.close()
#         conn.close()
#         return "Database connection successful!"
#     except Exception as e:
#         return f"Database connection failed: {e}"

# @app.route('/redis')
# def test_redis():
#     try:
#         r = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, db=0)
#         r.set('test_key', 'test_value')
#         value = r.get('test_key')
#         return f"Redis connection successful! Retrieved: {value.decode()}"
#     except Exception as e:
#         return f"Redis connection failed: {e}"

# if __name__ == '__main__':
#     app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=8080)

# supervisord.conf (place this file next to your Dockerfile)
# [supervisord]
# nodaemon=true

# [program:nginx]
# command=/usr/sbin/nginx -g "daemon off;"
# autostart=true
# autorestart=true
# priority=10

# [program:api]
# command=python3 /app/api_server.py
# directory=/app
# autostart=true
# autorestart=true
# priority=20

# [program:postgresql]
# command=su postgres -c "/usr/lib/postgresql/*/bin/pg_ctl -D /var/lib/postgresql/*/main start"
# user=postgres
# autostart=true
# autorestart=true
# priority=30
# stdout_logfile=/var/log/supervisor/postgresql.log
# stderr_logfile=/var/log/supervisor/postgresql_err.log
# # Need a custom start command for postgresql within supervisor
# # And ensure it's properly initialized or running in the Dockerfile
# # This needs a slightly more robust handling than a simple command.
# # For a true "all-in-one" dev container, ensure DB is running before app.

# [program:redis]
# command=/usr/bin/redis-server --daemonize no
# autostart=true
# autorestart=true
# priority=40
